content: A Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is a process designed to help organizations identify and minimize the data protection risks of a project. It is particularly relevant when processing personal data that may pose a high risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals.
source: ICO UK - Data Protection Impact Assessments

content: DPIAs are mandated under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for processing that is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals. Article 35 of the GDPR outlines the requirements for conducting a DPIA.

The significance of DPIAs lies in their ability to provide a structured approach to identifying risks and ensuring compliance with data protection laws, ultimately protecting individuals' privacy rights.
